western_philosophers.name: Thales of Miletus
western_philosophers.influence: First Western philosopher; founder of Milesian school

western_philosophers.name: Anaximander
western_philosophers.influence: Student of Thales; introduced the concept of apeiron

western_philosophers.name: Anaximenes
western_philosophers.influence: Last of the Milesian philosophers

western_philosophers.name: Pythagoras
key_ideas: Numbers as the essence of reality
western_philosophers.influence: Founded Pythagorean school; major influence on Plato and mathematics

western_philosophers.name: Xenophanes
western_philosophers.influence: Precursor of Eleatic school; early monotheist

western_philosophers.name: Heraclitus
western_philosophers.influence: Profound influence on Stoicism, Hegel, and process philosophy

western_philosophers.name: Parmenides
key_ideas: Way of Truth vs Way of Opinion
western_philosophers.influence: Founder of Eleatic school; major influence on Plato and metaphysics

western_philosophers.name: Zeno of Elea
key_ideas: Paradoxes of motion (Achilles and the tortoise)
western_philosophers.influence: Invented dialectic; paradoxes still discussed in mathematics and physics

western_philosophers.name: Empedocles
key_ideas: Four classical elements (earth, water, fire, air)
Love and Strife as cosmic forces
western_philosophers.influence: Four elements theory dominated Western thought for centuries

western_philosophers.name: Anaxagoras
western_philosophers.influence: Brought philosophy to Athens; influenced Socrates and Plato

western_philosophers.name: Leucippus
western_philosophers.influence: Co-founder of atomism with Democritus

western_philosophers.name: Democritus
western_philosophers.influence: Atomic theory anticipated modern physics; influenced Epicurus

western_philosophers.name: Protagoras
key_ideas: Man is the measure of all things
western_philosophers.influence: Most prominent Sophist; relativism still debated today

western_philosophers.name: Gorgias
key_ideas: Nothing can be known or communicated
western_philosophers.influence: Major Sophist; influenced rhetoric and skepticism

western_philosophers.name: Socrates
western_philosophers.influence: Central figure of Western philosophy; taught Plato; executed for impiety

western_philosophers.name: Plato
western_philosophers.influence: Whitehead called Western philosophy 'footnotes to Plato'; founded the Academy

western_philosophers.name: Aristotle
western_philosophers.influence: The Philosopher; foundational to logic, biology, ethics, politics, and metaphysics

western_philosophers.name: Pyrrho of Elis
western_philosophers.influence: Founder of Pyrrhonism; shaped ancient and modern skepticism

western_philosophers.name: Epicurus
western_philosophers.influence: Founded Epicureanism; major influence on Utilitarianism and secular ethics

western_philosophers.name: Zeno of Citium
western_philosophers.influence: Founder of Stoicism; shaped ethics and political thought

western_philosophers.name: Chrysippus
western_philosophers.influence: Second founder of Stoicism; systematized Stoic doctrine

western_philosophers.name: Cicero
western_philosophers.influence: Transmitted Greek philosophy to Rome and Latin West

western_philosophers.name: Lucretius
major_works: De Rerum Natura (On the Nature of Things)
western_philosophers.influence: Preserved Epicurean atomism; influenced scientific materialism

western_philosophers.name: Seneca
western_philosophers.influence: Major Stoic moralist; influenced Christianity and Renaissance humanism

western_philosophers.name: Epictetus
key_ideas: Dichotomy of control (what is up to us vs. not)
western_philosophers.influence: Profound influence on Stoic practice and modern cognitive therapy

western_philosophers.name: Marcus Aurelius
western_philosophers.influence: Philosopher-emperor; Meditations remains a Stoic classic

western_philosophers.name: Plotinus
key_ideas: Return to the One through contemplation
western_philosophers.influence: Founder of Neoplatonism; major influence on Augustine and medieval mysticism

western_philosophers.name: Porphyry
western_philosophers.influence: Transmitted Neoplatonism; Isagoge shaped medieval logic

western_philosophers.name: Augustine of Hippo
key_ideas: City of God vs. City of Man
major_works: On Free Choice of the Will
western_philosophers.influence: Most influential Christian philosopher; shaped Western theology and philosophy

western_philosophers.name: Boethius
western_philosophers.influence: Bridge between ancient and medieval philosophy; shaped scholasticism

western_philosophers.name: Anselm of Canterbury
western_philosophers.influence: Father of scholasticism; ontological argument still debated

western_philosophers.name: Peter Abelard
western_philosophers.influence: Pioneer of scholastic method; advanced logic in medieval philosophy

western_philosophers.name: Averroes (Ibn Rushd)
western_philosophers.influence: The Commentator on Aristotle; reintroduced Aristotle to Europe

western_philosophers.name: Maimonides
western_philosophers.influence: Greatest medieval Jewish philosopher; influenced Aquinas and Spinoza

western_philosophers.name: Roger Bacon
western_philosophers.influence: Precursor of scientific method and empiricism

western_philosophers.name: Thomas Aquinas
western_philosophers.influence: Doctor Angelicus; Catholic philosophical tradition; still official doctrine

western_philosophers.name: Meister Eckhart
western_philosophers.influence: Dominant figure of German mysticism; influenced German Idealism

western_philosophers.name: John Duns Scotus
western_philosophers.influence: Doctor Subtilis; influenced Scotism and Franciscan theology

western_philosophers.name: William of Ockham
western_philosophers.influence: Father of nominalism; Ockham's Razor is fundamental to scientific method

western_philosophers.name: Nicholas of Cusa
western_philosophers.influence: Bridge between medieval and Renaissance thought; influenced Giordano Bruno

western_philosophers.name: Niccolò Machiavelli
western_philosophers.influence: Father of modern political science; 'Machiavellian' entered the lexicon

western_philosophers.name: Erasmus
western_philosophers.influence: Prince of Humanists; influenced Reformation and Counter-Reformation

western_philosophers.name: Thomas More
western_philosophers.influence: Invented the word 'utopia'; influenced political philosophy and utopianism

western_philosophers.name: Michel de Montaigne
western_philosophers.influence: Invented the essay form; influenced Pascal, Descartes, and modern self-reflection

western_philosophers.name: Giordano Bruno
major_works: On the Infinite Universe and Worlds
western_philosophers.influence: Martyr for scientific thought; influenced cosmology and pantheism

western_philosophers.name: Francis Bacon
western_philosophers.influence: Father of empiricism; shaped scientific method and the Enlightenment

western_philosophers.name: Thomas Hobbes
key_ideas: State of nature (war of all against all)
western_philosophers.influence: Founder of modern political philosophy; influenced liberalism and absolutism

western_philosophers.name: René Descartes
western_philosophers.influence: Father of modern philosophy; Cartesian dualism; analytic geometry

western_philosophers.name: Blaise Pascal
western_philosophers.influence: Pioneered probability theory; Pascal's Wager in philosophy of religion

western_philosophers.name: Baruch Spinoza
western_philosophers.influence: Foundational for rationalism, pantheism, biblical criticism, and liberal democracy

western_philosophers.name: John Locke
western_philosophers.influence: Father of liberalism; foundational to US Declaration of Independence and human rights

western_philosophers.name: Nicolas Malebranche
western_philosophers.influence: Developed Cartesian system; influenced Leibniz and Berkeley

western_philosophers.name: Gottfried Wilhelm Leibniz
western_philosophers.influence: Co-inventor of calculus; rationalism; logic; influenced Kant and Hegel

western_philosophers.name: George Berkeley
key_ideas: Esse est percipi (to be is to be perceived)
major_works: A Treatise Concerning the Principles of Human Knowledge
western_philosophers.influence: Founder of idealism; influenced Kant, Hegel, and phenomenology

western_philosophers.name: Montesquieu
western_philosophers.influence: Foundational to constitutional government and separation of powers

western_philosophers.name: Voltaire
western_philosophers.influence: Leading Enlightenment figure; shaped secular liberalism

western_philosophers.name: David Hume
western_philosophers.influence: Awoke Kant from 'dogmatic slumber'; problem of induction still central

western_philosophers.name: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
western_philosophers.influence: Influenced French Revolution, Romanticism, and progressive education

western_philosophers.name: Denis Diderot
western_philosophers.influence: Led the Encyclopédie; spread Enlightenment ideas across Europe

western_philosophers.name: Jeremy Bentham
major_works: Introduction to the Principles of Morals and Legislation
western_philosophers.influence: Founder of Utilitarianism; influenced law reform and social policy

western_philosophers.name: Immanuel Kant
major_works: Groundwork for the Metaphysics of Morals
western_philosophers.influence: Copernican revolution in philosophy; shaped all subsequent philosophy

western_philosophers.name: Johann Gottfried Herder
major_works: Ideas on the Philosophy of the History of Humanity
This Too a Philosophy of History
western_philosophers.influence: Father of nationalism and historicism; influenced Romanticism

western_philosophers.name: Johann Gottlieb Fichte
western_philosophers.influence: First post-Kantian idealist; influenced Hegel and German nationalism

western_philosophers.name: Friedrich Wilhelm Joseph Schelling
western_philosophers.influence: Influenced Romanticism, Hegel, and later existentialism

western_philosophers.name: Georg Wilhelm Friedrich Hegel
western_philosophers.influence: Most influential post-Kantian philosopher; shaped Marx, existentialism, and 20th-century philosophy

western_philosophers.name: Arthur Schopenhauer
key_ideas: Compassion as the basis of ethics
major_works: The World as Will and Representation
western_philosophers.influence: Influenced Nietzsche, Freud, Wagner, and Eastern-Western philosophy dialogue

western_philosophers.name: August Comte
western_philosophers.influence: Father of positivism and sociology

western_philosophers.name: Ludwig Feuerbach
major_works: Principles of the Philosophy of the Future
western_philosophers.influence: Influenced Marx and Young Hegelians; critique of religion

western_philosophers.name: John Stuart Mill
western_philosophers.influence: Classical liberal; foundational to liberal democracy and utilitarian ethics

western_philosophers.name: Søren Kierkegaard
key_ideas: Three stages of existence (aesthetic, ethical, religious)
western_philosophers.influence: Father of existentialism; influenced Heidegger, Sartre, and Christian existentialism

western_philosophers.name: Karl Marx
western_philosophers.influence: Most politically influential philosopher; shaped 20th-century history and social theory

western_philosophers.name: Friedrich Engels
major_works: The Condition of the Working Class in England
western_philosophers.influence: Co-founder of Marxism with Marx

western_philosophers.name: Herbert Spencer
key_ideas: Survival of the fittest (coined the phrase)
western_philosophers.influence: Social Darwinism; influenced sociology and libertarianism

western_philosophers.name: Charles Sanders Peirce
major_works: How to Make Our Ideas Clear
western_philosophers.influence: Founder of pragmatism and semiotics

western_philosophers.name: Friedrich Nietzsche
western_philosophers.influence: One of most influential philosophers; shaped existentialism, postmodernism, and 20th century

western_philosophers.name: William James
western_philosophers.influence: Major figure in pragmatism and psychology of religion

western_philosophers.name: Gottlob Frege
western_philosophers.influence: Father of analytic philosophy and modern logic

western_philosophers.name: Edmund Husserl
western_philosophers.influence: Founder of phenomenology; shaped Heidegger, Sartre, Merleau-Ponty

western_philosophers.name: John Dewey
key_ideas: Democracy as a way of life
western_philosophers.influence: Shaped progressive education; major American pragmatist

western_philosophers.name: Henri Bergson
western_philosophers.influence: Nobel Prize in Literature; influenced process philosophy, Whitehead, and French thought

western_philosophers.name: Alfred North Whitehead
western_philosophers.influence: Founder of process philosophy; co-wrote Principia Mathematica with Russell

western_philosophers.name: Bertrand Russell
major_works: Why I Am Not a Christian
western_philosophers.influence: Co-founder of analytic philosophy; Nobel Prize in Literature; public intellectual

western_philosophers.name: G.E. Moore
western_philosophers.influence: Co-founder of analytic philosophy; naturalistic fallacy central to meta-ethics

western_philosophers.name: Ernst Cassirer
key_ideas: Myth, language, science as symbol systems
western_philosophers.influence: Neo-Kantian; philosophy of culture and symbolic forms

western_philosophers.name: Karl Jaspers
major_works: The Origin and Goal of History
western_philosophers.influence: Major German existentialist; introduced 'Axial Age' concept

western_philosophers.name: José Ortega y Gasset
key_ideas: Circumstantialism (I am I and my circumstance)
western_philosophers.influence: Most important 20th-century Spanish philosopher

western_philosophers.name: Martin Heidegger
western_philosophers.influence: Most influential 20th-century continental philosopher; shaped existentialism and postmodernism

western_philosophers.name: Ludwig Wittgenstein
key_ideas: Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent
western_philosophers.influence: One of most influential analytic philosophers; shaped philosophy of language

western_philosophers.name: Walter Benjamin
major_works: The Work of Art in the Age of Mechanical Reproduction
western_philosophers.influence: Critical theory; influenced media studies, cultural criticism, and Benjamin studies

western_philosophers.name: Max Horkheimer
western_philosophers.influence: Co-founder of Frankfurt School and Critical Theory

western_philosophers.name: Hannah Arendt
western_philosophers.influence: Major political philosopher; totalitarianism studies; public sphere

western_philosophers.name: Karl Popper
major_works: The Open Society and Its Enemies
western_philosophers.influence: Shaped philosophy of science; falsificationism criterion

western_philosophers.name: Theodor Adorno
western_philosophers.influence: Frankfurt School; critical theory; aesthetic philosophy

western_philosophers.name: Jean-Paul Sartre
western_philosophers.influence: Leading existentialist; engaged public intellectual; Nobel Prize declined

western_philosophers.name: Emmanuel Levinas
western_philosophers.influence: Ethics of alterity; major influence on post-Holocaust Jewish thought and ethics

western_philosophers.name: W.V.O. Quine
major_works: From a Logical Point of View
western_philosophers.influence: Most influential American analytic philosopher of 20th century

western_philosophers.name: Simone de Beauvoir
western_philosophers.influence: Mother of second-wave feminism; existentialist ethics

western_philosophers.name: Maurice Merleau-Ponty
western_philosophers.influence: Embodied cognition; phenomenological approach to psychology

western_philosophers.name: A.J. Ayer
western_philosophers.influence: Brought logical positivism to English-speaking world

western_philosophers.name: J.L. Austin
key_ideas: How to Do Things with Words
major_works: How to Do Things with Words
western_philosophers.influence: Founded speech act theory; ordinary language philosophy

western_philosophers.name: Albert Camus
western_philosophers.influence: Absurdism; Nobel Prize in Literature; engaged public intellectual

western_philosophers.name: Paul Ricoeur
western_philosophers.influence: Major hermeneutic philosopher; narrative theory of identity

western_philosophers.name: Donald Davidson
western_philosophers.influence: Major analytic philosopher of action, mind, and language

western_philosophers.name: Hans-Georg Gadamer
western_philosophers.influence: Founder of philosophical hermeneutics; lived to 102

western_philosophers.name: Herbert Marcuse
western_philosophers.influence: Frankfurt School; ideological father of 1968 student movements

western_philosophers.name: Gilbert Ryle
western_philosophers.influence: Ordinary language philosophy; critique of Cartesian dualism

western_philosophers.name: Peter Strawson
western_philosophers.influence: Major analytic philosopher; reactive attitudes central to free will debate

western_philosophers.name: John Rawls
western_philosophers.influence: Most influential political philosopher of the 20th century

western_philosophers.name: Thomas Kuhn
western_philosophers.influence: Transformed philosophy and history of science; 'paradigm shift' entered common usage

western_philosophers.name: Jean-François Lyotard
western_philosophers.influence: Defined postmodernism philosophically

western_philosophers.name: Gilles Deleuze
western_philosophers.influence: Major post-structuralist; influenced cultural studies, political theory

western_philosophers.name: Hilary Putnam
western_philosophers.influence: Major analytic philosopher of mind and language

western_philosophers.name: Michel Foucault
western_philosophers.influence: Most cited author in humanities; shaped cultural studies, gender theory, social sciences

western_philosophers.name: Louis Althusser
western_philosophers.influence: Structural Marxism; shaped critical theory and cultural studies

western_philosophers.name: Noam Chomsky
western_philosophers.influence: Father of modern linguistics; major political dissident intellectual

western_philosophers.name: Jürgen Habermas
major_works: The Structural Transformation of the Public Sphere
western_philosophers.influence: Leading Frankfurt School second generation; discourse ethics; public sphere theory

western_philosophers.name: Jean Baudrillard
key_ideas: The Gulf War Did Not Take Place
western_philosophers.influence: Postmodernism; media theory; consumer culture critique

western_philosophers.name: Jacques Derrida
western_philosophers.influence: Founder of deconstruction; shaped literary theory and humanities

western_philosophers.name: Richard Rorty
major_works: Philosophy and the Mirror of Nature
western_philosophers.influence: Post-analytic neo-pragmatism; bridged analytic and continental philosophy

western_philosophers.name: Félix Guattari
western_philosophers.influence: Post-structuralism; political philosophy

western_philosophers.name: Saul Kripke
major_works: Wittgenstein on Rules and Private Language
western_philosophers.influence: Revived metaphysics in analytic philosophy; modal logic semantics

western_philosophers.name: Daniel Dennett
western_philosophers.influence: Major philosopher of mind; popularized evolutionary view of consciousness

western_philosophers.name: Derek Parfit
western_philosophers.influence: Most important British moral philosopher of late 20th century

western_philosophers.name: Peter Singer
western_philosophers.influence: Shaped animal rights movement and effective altruism

western_philosophers.name: Slavoj Žižek
western_philosophers.influence: Most prominent contemporary continental philosopher and public intellectual

western_philosophers.name: Alain Badiou
western_philosophers.influence: Major contemporary French philosopher; set theory ontology

western_philosophers.name: Robert Nozick
western_philosophers.influence: Libertarian political philosophy; rebuttal to Rawls

western_philosophers.name: Michael Dummett
western_philosophers.influence: Major analytic philosopher; anti-realism in philosophy of language
